title: callista unguiculata
intro: Callista unguiculata, also known as the cowhage or cow itch, is a tropical plant native to the Indian Ocean region and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, thorny stem and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Callista unguiculata include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from skin conditions.
